demographics.living_situation: shares a residence with a housemate

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by his housemate) He woke up completely drenched in sweat, talking nonsense, and stared blankly when I asked what was wrong. He's not himself at all and I can't get a sensible answer out of him. Please help.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on overnight and he's getting worse — he's soaked in sweat, shaking, and now barely responding to me. He doesn't have any health conditions that I know of.

symptom_narrative.onset: found this way on waking overnight, after a long stretch without eating; the housemate says similar but milder spells have been happening for about three months
symptom_narrative.duration: this episode has been going for a while tonight and he is getting harder to rouse
symptom_narrative.character: drenching sweat, pale and clammy, a fine shaking, and confused, staring, saying things that make no sense; in the past he has described sudden intense hunger, sweating and a racing heart just before he goes confused
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the housemate says he has never seen him this bad — he can't hold a conversation or answer simple questions
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his whole body is sweaty and shaky and his mind is not right
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the spells tend to come on overnight or after exercise when he hasn't eaten for a while, and this one has kept getting worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: the housemate says eating something sweet has snapped him out of the milder spells before, but tonight he can't seem to eat or drink properly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: going without food, especially overnight or after exercise
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he has mentioned his vision going odd or blurry during some of the spells; tonight he is too confused to describe anything himself
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: roughly three months of increasingly frequent episodes of confusion and odd behaviour, usually when he has not eaten; he has been snacking constantly, including at night, to try to head them off
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: right now he cannot follow what is said to him, answer questions, or eat and drink safely; he is drowsy and dependent

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no known medical conditions; the housemate says he has never been told he has diabetes

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: has been eating very frequently, including snacks overnight, and has put on weight recently; the spells often follow exercise or a long gap without food
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: the housemate is present and made the call; there are discarded lolly/candy wrappers around his bed from him trying to fix the spells himself

objective_data_offered.value: he is soaked in sweat with his clothes wet through, very pale, agitated and staring blankly, and cannot answer me properly or eat and drink safely
his hands are trembling and he seems drowsy and hard to rouse
there are discarded lolly and chocolate wrappers around his bed where he has been trying to manage these spells himself
